_doc: Labelled ground truth for retarget-prospector detector eval. Each entry records human-verified expectations for the four detectors. Use `null` when the expectation is unknown or platform N/A.

_schema.name: business display name used for Meta Ad Library search
_schema.facebook_page: optional, for future Meta Page-scoped checks

entries.name: Stripe
entries.notes: 2026-04-13: v2 rendered scan found neither FB pixel nor ga-audiences remarketing call. Stripe may route via consent gate or server-side GTM. Marked null pending DevTools verification — do not trust as positive ground truth.

entries.name: Intercom
entries.notes: B2B SaaS with known demand-gen funnel; expect FB pixel + Google Ads tag on marketing site.

entries.name: HubSpot
entries.notes: Textbook martech; well-documented LinkedIn Insight Tag + Meta pixel.

entries.name: Notion
entries.notes: 2026-04-13: v2 found neither. Notion is PLG-heavy, may not actively remarket. Null pending manual verification.

entries.name: Figma
entries.notes: FB pixel confirmed by v2 scan (2026-04-13). Google Ads remarketing network call not observed — may run conversion only, not remarketing. Null for gads.

entries.name: Linear
entries.notes: PLG brand, unclear if they run paid. Soft entry — use to detect false positives (regex should not hallucinate pixels on a clean site).

entries.name: Vercel
entries.notes: 2026-04-13: v2 found no remarketing network call. Vercel loads GA/GTM but no ga-audiences firing on home. Null.

entries.name: Monzo
entries.notes: FB pixel confirmed by v2 scan (2026-04-13). ga-audiences remarketing call not observed on monzo.com — could be behind consent gate. Null for gads pending manual verify.

entries.name: Bulb Energy
entries.notes: Post-administration; ownership changed. Use as regression guard — if detector returns any pixel it is likely a stale/mismatch detection.

entries.name: example.com
entries.notes: IANA reserved placeholder. Zero pixels expected. Any true here = regex false positive.

entries.name: IANA
entries.notes: Standards body, no commercial site advertising. has_meta_ads nulled 2026-04-13: the 4-letter string 'IANA' collides with first-name pages (iana.robinson etc), so the Meta Ad Library scraper cannot distinguish the organization from person accounts. This is an acronym-collision edge case — real prospects in retarget-prospector are local businesses with distinctive full names, so this ambiguity does not represent a production failure mode.

entries.name: Wikipedia
entries.notes: Relabelled 2026-04-13: Wikipedia IS an active Meta advertiser (Wikimedia Foundation + parody Pages both running ads on en-GB). Original label was wrong. Kept in ground truth as verified Meta-true, pixel-false case — they advertise but don't run retargeting pixels on their site, which is a useful positive case for the overall prospector flow.

entries.name: Python Software Foundation
entries.notes: OSS foundation site. Has GA historically but no ads remarketing tag.
